finding: When `ATM_RUNNER_SYNC_REQUIRED` appears during a scoped task, the required `ATM_RETAIN_RELEASE_ARTIFACTS=1 npm run build` can leave release artifacts dirty while taskflow close classifies them as generated/advisory and excludes them from the task bundle. The task can close and push, but the operator is left with a dirty worktree after an otherwise successful card.
expectedBehavior: Taskflow should provide a governed release-artifact sync lane, explicit cleanup instruction, or scoped follow-up card when runner sync generates release artifacts during a task close path.
evidenceOrRepro: TASK-RFT-0068 close on 2026-07-16: `node atm.mjs tasks status --task TASK-RFT-0068 --json` first warned `ATM_RUNNER_SYNC_REQUIRED`; after `ATM_RETAIN_RELEASE_ARTIFACTS=1 npm run build`, taskflow close succeeded and pushed commits e4a824fd15bf54ec2ff0ff22b681ed965e8f1449 and 6af60833eaff15679385f6971902eaba8fd7dc1f, but `git status --short --branch` still showed modified `release/atm-onefile/*` and `release/atm-root-drop/*` files.
followUp: Add a governed release-sync closeout path or automatic follow-up task recommendation so required runner-sync artifacts do not remain as ambiguous dirty residue after a scoped task.
